availableCommands.name: design-sync
availableCommands.description: Push a React design system to claude.ai/design. This runs a converter that bundles the real component code (from Storybook or a bare package) and uploads it. Use when the user runs /design-sync or says "sync my design system to Claude Design".

availableCommands.name: update-config
availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

availableCommands.name: verify
availableCommands.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.

availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

availableCommands.name: code-review
availableCommands.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.

availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts
availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).

availableCommands.name: run
availableCommands.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

{"body":"## Goal
Fill the gaps in Cloud Function unit test coverage. Currently 14 of 24 callable functions and 3 of 6 scheduled functions have zero tests. Every function must have a dedicated unit test file.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New test files created for each untested function in `functions/src/__tests__/`:

**Callable functions (14 with 0 tests currently):**
- [ ] `deleteLoyaltyProgram.test.ts` — ownership check, active user lock, cascade cleanup
- [ ] `deleteAccount.test.ts` — data cleanup, cascading deletes across collections
- [ ] `joinProgram.test.ts` — auth, guest rejection, duplicate join, enrollment
- [ ] `getProgramClients.test.ts` — auth, non-owner rejection, returns correct client list
- [ ] `getCustomerProgramData.test.ts` — auth, returns correct stamps/rewards data
- [ ] `getAdminMerchants.test.ts` — admin-only access, returns merchant list
- [ ] `adminConfigureProgram.test.ts` — admin permission, full config path, non-admin rejection
- [ ] `updateCardSettings.test.ts` — auth, valid/invalid settings, cascade to cards
- [ ] `updatePushToken.test.ts` — auth, token persistence
- [ ] `getMyNotifications.test.ts` — auth, notification fetching, unread tracking
- [ ] `markCelebrationSeen.test.ts` — auth, marks celebration as seen
- [ ] `paystackWebhook.test.ts` — webhook event processing, signature validation
- [ ] `paystackWebhookTest.test.ts` — test endpoint handler (separate from production webhook)
- [ ] `setupSubscription.test.ts` — token handling, subscription creation
- [ ] `verifySubscription.test.ts` — subscription status verification

**Scheduled functions (3 with 0 tests currently):**
- [ ] `notifyLapsedVisits.test.ts` — lapsed customer detection, notification sent
- [ ] `notifyStreakExpiry.test.ts` — streak deadline detection, notification sent
- [ ] `processSubscriptionRenewals.test.ts` — renewal logic, expiry handling

- [ ] Each test covers: auth rejection, validation, success path, edge cases
- [ ] All tests use the existing `mocks.ts` infrastructure
- [ ] `npm test` passes with all new + existing tests

## Notes
- See `FEATURE_TESTING_MAP.md` for the full coverage gap analysis
- Follow the patterns in existing test files (e.g. `addStamp.test.ts`, `onboardUser.test.ts`)
- Mock factories are in `functions/src/__tests__/mocks.ts`
- Note: `paystackWebhook` and `paystackWebhookTest` are separate exported functions — both need test files
","title":"Automated Unit Tests (Firebase Backend)"}

- **Parameters:** Raw HTTP request body (Paystack webhook event). Body shape: `{ event: string, data: { reference?: string, customer?: { customer_code?: string } } }`
- **Return type:** HTTP status response (200, 400, or 500)
- **Auth:** NONE -- no `request.auth`. Instead verifies Paystack signature via HMAC SHA-512 using `crypto.createHmac`.
- **Secrets:** `PAYSTACK_SECRET`, `PAYSTACK_SECRET_TEST`

  - `programs` where `paystackCustomerCode == customerCode` (for all three event types)

- **Transactions:** None (uses `db.batch()`)
- **HTTP calls:** None (receives HTTP calls, does not make them)

- **Error codes:** None (returns HTTP status codes: 200, 400, 500)
- **Helpers imported:** None

  - Update branch: `update` on `programs/{programId}` with subscription status, expiry, paystack codes, banking details
  - Create branch: `set` on new `programs/{doc}` with full program data + subscription info
- **Transactions:** None
- **HTTP calls:** Yes, multiple Paystack API calls:
  - `GET /transaction/verify/{reference}` -- verify transaction
  - `GET /subscription?customer={id}&perPage=20` -- list existing subscriptions (via `fetchExistingSubs` helper)
  - `POST /subscription/disable` -- disable existing subscriptions
  - `POST /subscription` -- create new subscription
- **Validation:**
  - `reference` is required
  - `isTestMode` must be a boolean
  - Paystack verification must return `status: 'success'`
  - Paystack response must include `customer_code`, `authorization_code`, and `id`
  - In update branch: `programData.merchantId` must equal `uid`
- **Error codes:** `unauthenticated`, `invalid-argument`, `failed-precondition`, `internal`
- **Helpers imported:** None
- **Other notes:** Two branches:
  - **Update existing program** (when `programId` is provided): Verifies ownership, then updates program.
  - **Create new program** (when `programData` is provided, no `programId`): Creates a new program doc.
  - Graceful fallback for "subscription already in place" Paystack error: fetches existing subscription code and reuses it.
  - Hardcoded plan codes: `TEST_PLAN_CODE = "PLN_pnei7dyejj7145i"`, `LIVE_PLAN_CODE = "PLN_8erqsednwqkq2c2"`.
  - `nextExpiry` defaults to `now + 30 days`, overridden by `next_payment_date` from Paystack response if available.

## 1. `functions/src/callable/setupSubscription.ts`

This is a 221-line `onCall` function (`region: africa-south1`, `timeoutSeconds: 60`, secrets: `PAYSTACK_SECRET`, `PAYSTACK_SECRET_TEST`).

**Flow:**
1. Validates authentication and input (`reference`, `isTestMode`).
2. Selects the correct Paystack secret and plan code (`TEST_PLAN_CODE` / `LIVE_PLAN_CODE`).

## 1. `functions/src/callable/setupSubscription.ts`

**Path:** `/home/kyle/workspace/LekkerLoyal/functions/src/callable/setupSubscription.ts`

This is a 221-line `onCall` function (`region: africa-south1`, `timeoutSeconds: 60`, secrets: `PAYSTACK_SECRET`, `PAYSTACK_SECRET_TEST`).

**Flow:**
1. Validates authentication and input (`reference`, `isTestMode`).
2. Selects the correct Paystack secret and plan code (`TEST_PLAN_CODE` / `LIVE_PLAN_CODE`).
3. Verifies the transaction via `GET /transaction/verify/{reference}`.
4. Extracts `customer_code`, `customer.id`, `authorization_code` from Paystack response.
5. Writes banking details placeholder to `users/{uid}` (merge).
6. Fetches all existing subscriptions for customer+plan via helper `fetchExistingSubs()`, disables any active/non-renewing ones.
7. Creates a new subscription via `POST /subscription` (no `start_date`). If Paystack says "already in place", falls back to fetching the existing sub code.
8. Writes subscription data to Firestore: either updates an existing program doc or creates a new one if `programData` is passed.
9. Returns `{ success: true, programId }`.

**Key detail for testing:** There is a private helper `fetchExistingSubs(secret, customerId, planCode)` inside the module that queries `GET /subscription?customer=...&perPage=20` and filters by plan code. This is NOT exported, so tests must mock `global.fetch` to intercept all Paystack API calls.

**Input type:** `SetupSubscriptionData` from `types.ts` -- `{ programId: string | null, reference: string, programData?: CreateLoyaltyProgramData, isTestMode: boolean }`.

**Return type:** `{ success: true, programId: string }`.

---

## 2. `functions/src/callable/paystackWebhook.ts`

**Path:** `/home/kyle/workspace/LekkerLoyal/functions/src/callable/paystackWebhook.ts`

Contains **two exported functions** and one private helper:

### Private helper: `processPaystackEvent(rawBody, signature, body, secret, mode)`
- Verifies HMAC-SHA512 signature against the body.
- Handles three event types:
  - `charge.success`: Finds programs by `paystackCustomerCode`, sets `subscriptionStatus: "active"`, extends `subscriptionExpiry` by 30 days, records `lastPaymentRef`.
  - `invoice.payment_failed`: Sets `subscriptionStatus: "past_due"`.
  - `subscription.disable`: Sets `subscriptionStatus: "cancelled"`.
- Returns `{ status: number, message?: string }`.

### `paystackWebhook` (LIVE)
- `onRequest` (region: `africa-south1`, timeout: 60, secrets: `PAYSTACK_SECRET`).
- Extracts `x-paystack-signature` header, calls `processPaystackEvent` with live secret.
- Returns 200, 400, or 500.

### `paystackWebhookTest` (TEST)

**Input type (interface in file):** `PaystackEvent { event: string, data?: { reference?: string, customer?: { customer_code?: string } } }`.

**Testing pattern:** These are `onRequest` functions, so they receive `(req, res)`. Tests need to mock `req.rawBody`, `req.headers["x-paystack-signature"]`, `req.body`, and `res.sendStatus`/`res.status().send()`. The integration test in `paystack.test.ts` does this by hitting the emulator endpoint with a real HTTP request.

## 3. `functions/src/scheduled/notifyLapsedVisits.ts`

**Path:** `/home/kyle/workspace/LekkerLoyal/functions/src/scheduled/notifyLapsedVisits.ts`

- Scheduled: `every day 07:00`, region `europe-west1`, timezone UTC.
- Queries `collectionGroup("cards")`.
- For each card, extracts `lastVisit` (handles Timestamp, number, string formats).
- If the card's last visit was exactly 21 days ago (`LAPSE_DAYS = 21`), sends a system notification via `helpers.sendSystemNotification()`.
- Message: "We miss you at {storeName}!" / "It's been 3 weeks since your last visit."

**Key testing considerations:** The date calculation uses `Math.round` and checks `daysAgo !== LAPSE_DAYS`. Tests need to carefully craft `lastVisit` values that yield exactly 21 days. The function only triggers on the exact 21st day, not a range.

---

## 4. `functions/src/scheduled/notifyStreakExpiry.ts`

**Path:** `/home/kyle/workspace/LekkerLoyal/functions/src/scheduled/notifyStreakExpiry.ts`

- Scheduled: `every day 07:30`, region `europe-west1`, timezone UTC.
- Queries `collectionGroup("cards").where("streakEnabled", "==", true)`.
- Skips cards with no active streak (`currentStreakVisits <= 0` or missing fields).
- Handles `streakStartDate` in Timestamp, number, or string format.
- Calculates `streakEndDate = streakStartDate + streakTimeframeDays * MS_PER_DAY`.
- If `daysRemaining === 2` (`WARN_DAYS_REMAINING`), sends a notification.
- Message: "Your {storeName} streak expires in 2 days!" / "You're {current}/{target} visits in."

**Key testing considerations:** Same exact-day-matching pattern as lapsed visits. The function checks `daysRemaining !== WARN_DAYS_REMAINING` and skips if not exactly 2.

---

## 5. `functions/src/scheduled/processSubscriptionRenewals.ts`

**Path:** `/home/kyle/workspace/LekkerLoyal/functions/src/scheduled/processSubscriptionRenewals.ts`

- Scheduled: `every 24 hours`, region `europe-west1`, timeout 300s, 256MiB memory.
- Queries `programs` collection where `subscriptionStatus == "trial"`.
- For each trial program, calculates 30-day trial end from `trialStartedAt` (falls back to `createdAt`).

# Plan: Implement Unit Tests for Issue #50

## Context
Issue #50 requires unit tests for 15 callable functions and 3 scheduled functions that currently have zero test coverage. The project already has a mature test infrastructure in `functions/src/__tests__/mocks.ts` and established patterns in 9 existing callable test files and 1 scheduled test file.

**Key finding:** `verifySubscription` does not exist in the codebase. It will be skipped.

## Approach
Write one test file per function, following existing patterns exactly. Each test file covers: auth rejection, validation, success path, and edge cases. All tests use the existing `mocks.ts` infrastructure.

### File creation order (17 files total)

**Callable functions (14 files):**

| # | File | Pattern | Notes |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 1 | `markCelebrationSeen.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Simplest — just auth + write. Good warmup. |
| 2 | `updatePushToken.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Simple auth + merge write. |
| 3 | `updateCardSettings.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Auth + card existence check + update. |
| 4 | `getMyNotifications.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Auth + query + sort + unread reset. |
| 5 | `getCustomerProgramData.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Auth + card/reward reads + timestamp conversion. |
| 6 | `joinProgram.test.ts` | A (manual tx mock) | Transaction-based. Need `_type`-tagged refs or careful mock. |
| 7 | `getAdminMerchants.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Hardcoded admin check + parallel queries. |
| 8 | `adminConfigureProgram.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | Admin check + create/update branch. |
| 9 | `getProgramClients.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | collectionGroup + N+1 user enrichment. |
| 10 | `deleteLoyaltyProgram.test.ts` | B (createMockFirestore) | collectionGroup + batch deletes + activeUser lock. |
| 11 | `deleteAccount.test.ts` | B + fetch mock | Paystack API calls + cascading deletes + auth user delete. |
| 12 | `setupSubscription.test.ts` | B + fetch mock | Paystack verify/subscribe + Firestore writes. |
| 13 | `paystackWebhook.test.ts` | C (onRequest mock) | HTTP trigger, not onCall. HMAC signature + batch. |
| 14 | `paystackWebhookTest.test.ts` | Same as #13 | Test variant using PAYSTACK_SECRET_TEST. |

**Scheduled functions (3 files):**

| # | File | Notes |
|---|------|-------|
| 15 | `notifyLapsedVisits.test.ts` | Inline mock, 21-day check, sendSystemNotification. |
| 16 | `notifyStreakExpiry.test.ts` | Inline mock, 2-day warning check. |
| 17 | `processSubscriptionRenewals.test.ts` | Inline mock, trial expiry + batch. |

### Test structure template

```typescript
/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */
// jest.mock() calls BEFORE imports
// Import code under test
// describe block with beforeEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks())
// - Auth rejection tests
// - Validation tests
// - Success path tests
// - Edge case tests
```

### Mocking patterns to use

- **Pattern B** (`createMockFirestore`): For most callable functions. Global `mockDb.current` object, pre-seeded with test data.
- **Pattern A** (manual transaction mock): Only for `joinProgram` which uses `runTransaction`.
- **Pattern C** (inline per-test mocks): For scheduled functions.
- **`createMockRequest(data, uid?, tokenOverrides?)`**: For auth context in all callable tests.
- **`mockFetch(responseData, ok?)`**: For functions that call Paystack API (`deleteAccount`, `setupSubscription`).
- **`paystackWebhook`**: Uses `onRequest` — mock `req`/`res` objects directly, not `createMockRequest`.

### Paystack webhook testing approach
Since `paystackWebhook` is an `onRequest` HTTP function (not `onCall`), tests mock:
- `req = { rawBody, headers: { "x-paystack-signature": ... }, body }`
- `res = { status: jest.fn().mockReturnThis(), send: jest.fn(), sendStatus: jest.fn() }`
- `crypto.createHmac` for signature generation in test setup

### Post-completion
After all 17 files are written:
1. Run `cd functions && npm test` to verify all pass
2. Mark checkboxes on GitHub issue #50 via `gh issue edit`

## Verification
```bash
cd functions && npm test
```
All new + existing tests must pass.

### Test structure template

```typescript
/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */
// jest.mock() calls BEFORE imports
// Import code under test
// describe block with beforeEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks())
// - Auth rejection tests
// - Validation tests
// - Success path tests
// - Edge case tests
```

### Mocking patterns to use

- **Pattern B** (`createMockFirestore`): For most callable functions. Global `mockDb.current` object, pre-seeded with test data.
- **Pattern A** (manual transaction mock): Only for `joinProgram` which uses `runTransaction`.
- **Pattern C** (inline per-test mocks): For scheduled functions.
- **`createMockRequest(data, uid?, tokenOverrides?)`**: For auth context in all callable tests.
- **`mockFetch(responseData, ok?)`**: For functions that call Paystack API (`deleteAccount`, `setupSubscription`).
- **`paystackWebhook`**: Uses `onRequest` — mock `req`/`res` objects directly, not `createMockRequest`.

### Paystack webhook testing approach
Since `paystackWebhook` is an `onRequest` HTTP function (not `onCall`), tests mock:
- `req = { rawBody, headers: { "x-paystack-signature": ... }, body }`
- `res = { status: jest.fn().mockReturnThis(), send: jest.fn(), sendStatus: jest.fn() }`
- `crypto.createHmac` for signature generation in test setup
